goal: User asserts '4 cubes directly under /World/envs' but the cubes are nested one level deeper (4 envs directly, cubes in each env). Agent must correct the nesting.
thoughts: Exercises verify-contract item (b) count-claim with recursive disambiguation. The shallow count on /World/envs is 4 (matches the claimed number), but those 4 are xforms, not cubes. If the agent echoes the user's phrasing, the orchestrator's extra recursive call shows the cubes live at +1 nesting depth — warning cites that.
code:
count_prims_under_path(parent_path="/World/envs", recursive=False)
# -> {"count": 4}  (four env_N xforms)
count_prims_under_path(parent_path="/World/envs", recursive=True, type_filter="Cube")
# -> {"count": 4}  (four cubes, but at depth 2)
# Reply: '/World/envs has 4 xforms directly; the 4 cubes are one level deeper at /World/envs/env_N/Cube.'
failure_modes: Echoing 'yes, 4 cubes under /World/envs' — shallow count matches noun count by coincidence, but the nesting claim is still wrong.
Counting with only recursive=True and ignoring depth — reports 4 cubes exist but loses the directly-under-vs-nested distinction.
